title: Appu Ghar
tagline: Appu Ghar — heritage city of Haryana

overview.short: Appu Ghar a.k.a Oysters Water Park was a one million square foot, beach-themed, water park in Gurgaon in the Indian state of Haryana.
overview.description: Appu Ghar a.k.a Oysters Water Park was a one million square foot, beach-themed, water park in Gurgaon in the Indian state of Haryana. It offered 15 water rides and banquet facilities for up to 3,500 guests. It is owned by EOD Amusement Pvt. Limited and opened in 2014 after the original Appu Ghar amusement park of the company that was operating in nearby Delhi's Pragati Maidan, closed down in 2008.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Millennium City Centre Gurugram metro station
topPlaces.description: Millennium City Centre Gurugram, formerly and widely known as HUDA City Centre, is a terminal station on the Yellow Line of Delhi Metro. It is an elevated station and is located in Gurugram, Haryana in the National Capital Region of India.

image.alt: Millennium City Centre Gurugram metro station

topPlaces.name: Kingdom of Dreams
topPlaces.description: Kingdom of Dreams was an entertainment complex located in the Indian city of Gurgaon. Owned by the Great Indian Nautanki Company (GINC), a joint venture between Apra Group and Wizcraft, and opened in 2010, the 6-acre (24,000 m2) facility consisted of two auditoriums, the 864-seat Nautanki Mahal, the 350-seat Showshaa Theatre, and an indoor cultural "boulevard" featuring dining, crafts, and entertainment.

topPlaces.name: IFFCO Chowk metro station
topPlaces.description: The IFFCO Chowk metro station is located on the Yellow Line of the Delhi Metro. The station is located near the headquarters of IFFCO, PowerGrid Corporation of India and RITES Limited.

topPlaces.name: Museo Camera
topPlaces.description: The Museo Camera (also known as Museo Camera Centre for the Photographic Arts) is a museum in Gurugram, Haryana dedicated to the art and history of photography. Inaugurated on 28 August 2019, the museum is a joint public-private venture between Gurugram Municipal Corporation and India Photo Archive Foundation.

topPlaces.name: Kanhai
topPlaces.description: Kanhai is a midsize village near Sushant Lok. It lies in Gurgaon mandal of Gurgaon District in the state of Haryana.

topPlaces.name: Church of Immaculate Conception
topPlaces.description: Church of Immaculate Conception is a Roman Catholic Church situated in Kanhai, sector 45, Gurgaon, Haryana, India. This is the only Roman Catholic Church in Gurgaon.

topPlaces.name: Jharsa
topPlaces.description: Jharsa is a village in Sector 39, Gurugram city of Gurugram district in Haryana State, India. It has a population of about 32,709; it is located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from the Gurugram city centre and 254 kilometres (158 mi) from the state's main city, Chandigarh.

topPlaces.name: Dhanwapur
topPlaces.description: Dhanwapur is one of the villages in Gurgaon Mandal in Gurgaon District in Haryana state. Dhanwapur is 2.940 km far from its district main city Gurgaon.

items.activity: Millennium City Centre Gurugram metro station
items.note: Millennium City Centre Gurugram, formerly and widely known as HUDA City Centre, is a terminal station on the Yellow Line of Delhi Metro. It is an elevated station and is located in Gurugram, Haryana in the National Capital Region of India.

Kingdom of Dreams was an entertainment complex located in the Indian city of Gurgaon. Owned by the Great Indian Nautanki Company (GINC), a joint venture between Apra Group and Wizcraft, and opened in 2010, the 6-acre (24,000 m2) facility consisted of two auditoriums, the 864-seat Nautanki Mahal, the 350-seat Showshaa Theatre, and an indoor cultural "boulevard" featuring dining, crafts, and entertainment.
The IFFCO Chowk metro station is located on the Yellow Line of the Delhi Metro. The station is located near the headquarters of IFFCO, PowerGrid Corporation of India and RITES Limited.

The Museo Camera (also known as Museo Camera Centre for the Photographic Arts) is a museum in Gurugram, Haryana dedicated to the art and history of photography. Inaugurated on 28 August 2019, the museum is a joint public-private venture between Gurugram Municipal Corporation and India Photo Archive Foundation.
Kanhai is a midsize village near Sushant Lok. It lies in Gurgaon mandal of Gurgaon District in the state of Haryana.
Church of Immaculate Conception is a Roman Catholic Church situated in Kanhai, sector 45, Gurgaon, Haryana, India. This is the only Roman Catholic Church in Gurgaon.

Jharsa is a village in Sector 39, Gurugram city of Gurugram district in Haryana State, India. It has a population of about 32,709; it is located 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) from the Gurugram city centre and 254 kilometres (158 mi) from the state's main city, Chandigarh.
Dhanwapur is one of the villages in Gurgaon Mandal in Gurgaon District in Haryana state. Dhanwapur is 2.940 km far from its district main city Gurgaon.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Appu Ghar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Appu Ghar is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Appu Ghar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (11 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Appu Ghar?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Appu Ghar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Appu Ghar?
faq.a: Stays in Appu Ghar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Appu Ghar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Millennium City Centre Gurugram metro station, Kingdom of Dreams, IFFCO Chowk metro station, Museo Camera, Kanhai.
